posts.author: Finopoly
posts.text: Fund AUM duplication — No.It may look double counted because one fund puts money into another but in total AMFI remove this overlap, so actual money isn’t counted twice.

posts.author: tarun2
posts.text: Fund AUM duplication — If Mutual fund scheme say Parag Parikh Conservative Hybrid fund owns Liquid fund of the same house, does it not inflate the AUM, as it is counted twice?
